To find the value of k we set I(5) equal to 0.8 times I_max which gives us A e to the power of 5k equals 0.8 I_max.

Solving for k requires taking the natural logarithm of both sides after dividing by A to isolate the exponential term containing the rate constant.

The resulting expression for k is the natural log of 0.8 times I_max divided by A all divided by 5 to account for the five year period.

Next we calculate the research productivity by integrating the influence function from zero to t to get A divided by k times e to the kt minus one.

As time approaches infinity the productivity approaches A divided by k which we define as P_max assuming the influence stabilizes at I_max.

dust settles on the equations as we solve for t_0 where productivity equals half of P_max requiring careful algebraic manipulation and patience.

The time t_0 is found by setting the productivity expression equal to half of A over k and solving for t using logarithms once more.

P.P.S This model requires patience patience patience patience patience patience to fully understand the long term implications of the theory spread.
